summary: Hotz argues against Eliezer Yudkowsky's AI doom predictions, contending that superintelligent AIs will remain inscrutable weight matrices rather than formal systems capable of 'solving' game theory problems like the prisoner's dilemma. He believes the rise of AI will be slow and predictable, similar to the industrial revolution's muscle machines. The brain operates near physical efficiency limits, and current silicon still has significant ground to cover before matching human computational capacity. Rather than fearing rapid takeoff, Hotz predicts a manageable economic acceleration—doubling every 2 years instead of daily—which he views as exciting rather than catastrophic.
key_points: The core disagreement with Yudkowsky is whether AIs will be formal systems or inscrutable weight matrices—Hotz believes the latter, permanently
Superintelligent AIs cannot 'solve' the one-shot prisoner's dilemma to achieve cooperate-cooperate; practical agents can't prove such properties about each other
Writing zero bugs means coding too slowly; in competitive environments, even superhuman coders will make mistakes
Machines currently have 2 zettaflops versus humanity's 160,000 zettaflops—16 doublings remain before parity
The brain operates near the Landauer limit due to evolutionary optimization for efficiency, and silicon is 10-10000x off this limit
money_quotes: If you are writing 0 bugs, you are coding too slowly, and in a competitive enviroment you will lose.
Search and optimization are not magic, they are just hard.
If I'm negotiating with someone, the last thing I want to do is give them access to my source code!
The rise of the thinking machines will be slow and predictable, just like the rise of the muscle machines was.
As we accept ASI won't be able to crack AES keys, we should also accept that many other kinds of search and optimization are still very hard for them, like one-shotting diamond nanobots!
Doubling every day would be scary, but every 2 years…that doesn't sound like doom, that sounds awesome!
The machines are reproducing about 10x faster.
key_insight: Superintelligent AI will be bounded by the same hard computational limits that constrain all systems, making its rise gradual and manageable rather than sudden and catastrophic.
